prompt:
SRE tooling request. Our Kafka Connect fleet degrades quietly: a sink task hits a poison record, flips to FAILED, and nobody notices until the lag alert two hours later, because Connect never restarts failed tasks by itself. On-call then restarts entire connectors by hand, which also bounces the healthy tasks. I want a monitor library our .NET ops runner can call: sweep the cluster, restart precisely what's failed, and hand me a deterministic report I can diff between runs.

Ground rules: before changing code, use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Kafka Connect REST interface docs on docs.confluent.io (the REST reference and the monitoring page). Confirm how ?expand=status&expand=info works on GET /connectors, the status/tasks response shapes, the restart endpoint's includeTasks/onlyFailed query parameters with their response codes (202 vs 204 vs 409), and the documented 409-during-rebalance behavior. The pinned contract is docs/contract.json, provenance docs/official_sources.json - protected, like the test files. If the current official docs disagree with the fixture, report the conflict in your final summary instead of editing protected files. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final response.

Build (namespace ConnectMonitor, wired for the xunit harness in ConnectMonitorTests.cs):

- ConnectClient(baseUrl, username, password, HttpClient): ListConnectorsAsync (ONE call with both expands - don't fan out per-connector status GETs), GetStatusAsync, RestartFailedAsync using the researched query parameters. Basic auth on every request, Accept application/json, Connect's {error_code, message} envelope surfaced as ConnectApiException with the HTTP status, and any 3xx treated as an error - never follow a redirect with credentials.
- Records: ConnectTaskStatus(Id, State, WorkerId, Trace), ConnectorStatus(Name, State, WorkerId, Type, Tasks), TaskLine, RemediationAction(Connector, Action, Attempts, Result), RemediationReport(Tasks, Actions, Healthy), RestartOutcome - shapes as the tests use them.
- RemediationMonitor(client, maxConflictRetries, conflictBackoff): RunAsync sweeps once and returns the report. Flatten every task state (connectors ordinal by name, tasks by id). Restart ONLY connectors that are FAILED or have a FAILED task, action RESTART_FAILED_TASKS, results ACCEPTED/NO_CONTENT. A 409 (rebalance) retries the same restart through the injected backoff delegate - no Task.Delay - up to maxConflictRetries, then records REBALANCE_DEFERRED and keeps sweeping. A connector deleted mid-sweep (404) records NOT_FOUND and doesn't abort the run. Healthy connectors (RUNNING, PAUSED) are listed untouched. Same cluster state in, byte-identical report out.

Verify with: dotnet test. ConnectMonitorTests.cs, ConnectMonitor.csproj, .gitignore and docs/ are protected - don't modify them. Split the implementation into sensible files (client / monitor / models).

research.interface: Kafka Connect REST interface, worker port 8083, application/json (Confluent Platform, current docs)

official_sources.title: Kafka Connect REST Interface for Confluent Platform
official_sources.used_for: GET /connectors with ?expand=status and ?expand=info response shapes; GET /connectors/{name}/status with connector state/worker_id and tasks id/state/worker_id/trace; POST /connectors/{name}/restart with includeTasks and onlyFailed query parameters (defaults false), response codes 200/202/204/404/409/500 and the 202 body with state RESTARTING; POST /connectors/{name}/tasks/{taskId}/restart; 409 semantics while a worker-group rebalance is in process; application/json as the only supported entity content type

official_sources.title: Monitor Kafka Connect and Connectors for Confluent Platform
official_sources.used_for: the connector/task state model (UNASSIGNED, RUNNING, PAUSED, FAILED), the fact that Connect does not automatically restart failed tasks so states diverge, failure details surfacing in the status output, and the restart endpoints recommended for recovering failed connectors/tasks

research.language_rationale: Kafka Connect has no official C# management SDK; its documented management surface is the JSON REST interface, and C# with HttpClient/System.Text.Json is the idiomatic stack for an ops monitor embedded in the team's existing .NET tooling.
